title: #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)] on handle_state_change is justified: struct extraction would add lifetime complexity without benefit
description: handle_state_change takes 8 parameters. The allow is genuinely justified, not masking a design smell. The parameters are: a cloned Arc (client), two borrowed &str slices (api_url, account_id), a borrowed HashMap reference (changed), a borrowed Option<String> (current_chat), a mutably borrowed String (chat_state), a borrowed SyncSender (tx), and a borrowed egui::Context (ctx). These span four different lifetime groups and include a &mut borrow. A context struct would require either multiple lifetime parameters or owned clones (allocation). The function is only called from one callsite in the main loop where all these values are already in scope. The allow is appropriate; the alternative is strictly worse.
